changeSet.author: nexus-e0hd2
changeSet.comment: chash_index: normalize legacy 64-char rows to the [:32] natural id BEFORE constraining (RDR-108 D1)
changeSet.sql: -- The SQLite-era chash_index could hold FULL 64-hex chashes (the citation -- lineage width — RDR-180 G1), and migrate_chash_rows copies VERBATIM, so -- migrated tenants may carry them (the read side has always compensated -- with substr(chash,1,32) — ChashRepository.registeredChashesForCollection). -- Normalize to the [:32] chunk-id form the rest of the system keys on. -- Dedupe first: a truncated row may collide with an existing 32-char row -- on the (tenant_id, chash, physical_collection) natural key. DELETE FROM nexus.chash_index a WHERE length(a.chash) = 64 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM nexus.chash_index b WHERE b.tenant_id = a.tenant_id AND b.physical_collection = a.physical_collection AND b.chash = substr(a.chash, 1, 32)); -- Second collision class: two 64-char rows sharing a [:32] prefix would -- collide with EACH OTHER mid-UPDATE — keep one per prefix (lowest ctid). DELETE FROM nexus.chash_index a WHERE length(a.chash) = 64 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM nexus.chash_index b WHERE b.tenant_id = a.tenant_id AND b.physical_collection = a.physical_collection AND length(b.chash) = 64 AND substr(b.chash, 1, 32) = substr(a.chash, 1, 32) AND b.ctid < a.ctid); UPDATE nexus.chash_index SET chash = substr(chash, 1, 32) WHERE length(chash) = 64;

changeSet.author: nexus-e0hd2
changeSet.comment: chash_index: ADD the missing length(chash)=32 CHECK (NOT VALID first, same catalog-002 discipline)
changeSet.sql: ALTER TABLE nexus.chash_index ADD CONSTRAINT chash_index_chash_len_check CHECK (length(chash) = 32) NOT VALID;
changeSet.rollback: ALTER TABLE nexus.chash_index DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS chash_index_chash_len_check

changeSet.author: nexus-1wjmq
changeSet.comment: chash_index: RE-RUN the 013-0 normalization with FORCE RLS toggled off — 013-0 silently no-op'd in production (2026-07-08 v0.1.33 deploy incident)
changeSet.sql: -- INCIDENT nexus-1wjmq (2026-07-08, conexus-ml1z): the v0.1.33 cloud deploy -- failed at 013-2's VALIDATE. Root cause: nexus_admin (the Liquibase role) -- is the table OWNER but has NO BYPASSRLS, and chash-001 put -- FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY on chash_index — so 013-0's DELETE/UPDATE saw -- ZERO rows (RLS filtered everything; FORCE applies even to the owner), -- while VALIDATE (DDL — never row-filtered) saw the un-normalized legacy -- 64-char rows and failed. Tests missed it because Testcontainers runs -- Liquibase as the superuser (implicit BYPASSRLS). -- -- Fix: toggle FORCE off for the duration of this changeset (the OWNER then -- bypasses policies; the serving role is NOT the owner, so tenant isolation -- is unaffected throughout), re-run the exact 013-0 normalization -- (idempotent — dedupe classes delete nothing when absent, the UPDATE -- matches nothing once normalized), and restore FORCE. 013-0/013-1 are -- left untouched (checksum immutability: both are already recorded as -- executed on the affected tenants). -- -- Lock strength (critique): the FORCE toggles take ACCESS EXCLUSIVE on -- chash_index for this changeset's (brief) transaction — stricter than -- 013-0's ROW EXCLUSIVE and 013-2's SHARE UPDATE EXCLUSIVE. Acceptable: -- boot-time migration window, residual-legacy-rows workload only. ALTER TABLE nexus.chash_index NO FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; DELETE FROM nexus.chash_index a WHERE length(a.chash) = 64 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM nexus.chash_index b WHERE b.tenant_id = a.tenant_id AND b.physical_collection = a.physical_collection AND b.chash = substr(a.chash, 1, 32)); DELETE FROM nexus.chash_index a WHERE length(a.chash) = 64 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM nexus.chash_index b WHERE b.tenant_id = a.tenant_id AND b.physical_collection = a.physical_collection AND length(b.chash) = 64 AND substr(b.chash, 1, 32) = substr(a.chash, 1, 32) AND b.ctid < a.ctid); UPDATE nexus.chash_index SET chash = substr(chash, 1, 32) WHERE length(chash) = 64; ALTER TABLE nexus.chash_index FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

changeSet.author: nexus-e0hd2
changeSet.comment: VALIDATE all five chash length CHECKs (chunks tables: A-1 residue 0; chash_index: safe via the 013-0 normalization — A-1 never audited it; SHARE UPDATE EXCLUSIVE only)
